intermediate.title: A(n), the, no article
intermediate.content:
📌 A / AN – Dùng khi nào?
1. Đề cập lần đầu (first mention):
I saw an old woman with a dog in the park.

2. Nghề nghiệp / loại người hoặc vật:
Paula is a teacher. That's a chimpanzee. When I was a teenager, I enjoyed sleeping.

3. Tỉ lệ / tần suất (rate):
I work 6 hours a day. The rent is €500 a month. We were driving at 70 km an hour.

4. Cấu trúc what a / such a + danh từ đếm được số ít:
What a fantastic idea! Yesterday was such a horrible day!

❗ Lưu ý: Không dùng danh từ đếm được số ít một mình (không có a/the/my...):
❌ I have car. → ✅ I have a car.
❌ She is teacher. → ✅ She is a teacher.
📌 THE – Dùng khi nào?
1. Đề cập lần hai (second mention) hoặc đã biết là cái nào:
I saw a man and a woman. The man was British.
Can you close the window? (= cái cửa sổ đó – chúng ta đều biết)

2. Những nơi thường lui tới trong thành phố:
I'm going to the bank / the cinema / the doctor / the park / the station.

3. Trước tính từ so sánh nhất (superlative):
This is the best restaurant in town.

4. Tên đại dương, biển, sông, kênh đào:
the Mediterranean, the Amazon, the Panama Canal

5. Trước tên nhạc cụ:
Margaret plays the guitar.

6. Home / work / bed / school / university / hospital (nghĩa chức năng):
David isn't at school this morning. (= đang học) / She has been in bed all morning.
⚠️ Nhưng dùng the khi đến thăm (không phải chức năng): I went to the school to meet his teacher.
📌 NO ARTICLE (Zero Article) – Không dùng mạo từ khi nào?
1. Danh từ số nhiều hoặc không đếm được – nói chung chung:
Love and health are more important than money. I love music. Women drive more cautiously than men.
So sánh: I love music. (chung chung) ↔ I loved the music at the party. (cụ thể)

2. Tên riêng, quốc gia, thành phố, ngôn ngữ, môn học:
I live in Vietnam. She studies English and History.

3. Home, work, bed, school, university, hospital (nghĩa chức năng – xem bên trên):
She goes to university. He is in hospital.

4. Bữa ăn, môn thể thao, màu sắc (nói chung):
We have lunch at noon. He plays football.
📐 Bảng so sánh nhanh
Tình huốngVí dụ
Đề cập lần đầuI bought a new phone.
Đề cập lần hai / biết cái nàoThe phone was expensive.
Chung chung (số nhiều / không đếm được)I love – coffee. – Dogs are loyal.
Nghề nghiệpShe is a doctor.
Nhạc cụHe plays the piano.
Tên riêng / quốc giaShe lives in – France.

exercises.q: She wants to be ___ architect when she grows up.
exercises.exp: an + nghề nghiệp bắt đầu bằng nguyên âm → an architect

exercises.q: I bought ___ new laptop yesterday. ___ laptop is really fast.
exercises.exp: Lần đầu đề cập → a; lần hai → The

exercises.q: ___ Mount Everest is ___ highest mountain in the world.
exercises.exp: Tên riêng không dùng mạo từ; highest = superlative → the

exercises.q: Can you play ___ piano?
exercises.exp: Tên nhạc cụ → the piano

exercises.q: I don't drink ___ coffee. It keeps me awake.
exercises.exp: Danh từ không đếm được nói chung chung → không dùng mạo từ

exercises.q: She has been in ___ hospital for three days.
exercises.exp: in hospital = đang điều trị (nghĩa chức năng) → không dùng mạo từ

exercises.q: I went to ___ hospital to visit my friend.
exercises.exp: Đến thăm (không phải bệnh nhân) → the hospital

exercises.q: What ___ fantastic idea! You're so creative!
exercises.exp: What a/an + danh từ đếm được số ít → What a fantastic idea

exercises.q: The rent is €800 ___ month.
exercises.exp: Tỉ lệ → a month (= per month)

exercises.q: ___ love is more important than ___ money.
exercises.exp: Khái niệm trừu tượng nói chung → không dùng mạo từ

exercises.q: She studied ___ biology at ___ Cambridge University.
exercises.exp: Môn học và tên trường đại học tên riêng → không dùng mạo từ

exercises.q: I'm going to ___ bank. Do you need anything while I'm out?
exercises.exp: Những nơi thường lui tới trong thành phố → the bank

exercises.q: This is ___ best coffee I've ever tasted.
exercises.exp: Superlative → the best

exercises.q: She plays ___ tennis every Saturday morning.
exercises.exp: Môn thể thao → không dùng mạo từ

exercises.q: He left ___ home at seven and arrived at ___ office at eight.
exercises.exp: home (chức năng, không mạo từ); the office (cụ thể, đã biết)

exercises.q: ___ Amazon is ___ longest river in South America.
exercises.exp: Tên sông → The Amazon; superlative → the longest

exercises.q: I saw ___ interesting documentary last night. ___ documentary was about climate change.
exercises.exp: Lần đầu → an (nguyên âm); lần hai → The

exercises.q: She is ___ honest person who never lies.
exercises.exp: an + tính từ bắt đầu bằng nguyên âm (honest → /ɒnɪst/) → an honest

exercises.q: My brother is at ___ university. He's studying law.
exercises.exp: at university (chức năng = đang học) → không dùng mạo từ

exercises.q: We had ___ dinner at ___ nice Italian restaurant last night.
exercises.exp: Bữa ăn nói chung → không dùng; lần đầu đề cập nhà hàng → a

exercises.q: I need ___ (a/an/the/–) new umbrella. I lost mine yesterday.
exercises.exp: Đề cập lần đầu, danh từ đếm được số ít → a

exercises.q: ___ (a/an/the/–) children need love and attention to grow up well.
exercises.exp: Children nói chung chung → không dùng mạo từ

exercises.q: She plays ___ (a/an/the/–) violin in a local orchestra.
exercises.exp: Tên nhạc cụ → the violin

exercises.q: I go to ___ (a/an/the/–) gym three times ___ (a/an/the/–) week.
exercises.exp: the gym (cụ thể, đã biết); a week = per week (tỉ lệ)

exercises.q: He's ___ (a/an/the/–) engineer at ___ (a/an/the/–) small tech company.
exercises.exp: an + nghề nghiệp (vowel sound); a + đề cập lần đầu

exercises.q: It was such ___ (a/an/the/–) boring meeting that I almost fell asleep.
exercises.exp: such a + danh từ đếm được số ít → such a boring meeting

exercises.q: ___ (a/an/the/–) Pacific Ocean is ___ (a/an/the/–) largest ocean on Earth.
exercises.exp: Tên đại dương → The Pacific; superlative → the largest

exercises.q: She was in ___ (a/an/the/–) prison for two years.
exercises.exp: in prison (chức năng = bị giam) → không dùng mạo từ

exercises.q: My sister is ___ (a/an/the/–) doctor. She works at ___ (a/an/the/–) hospital near our house.
exercises.exp: Nghề nghiệp → a doctor; lần đầu đề cập bệnh viện → a hospital

exercises.q: I don't eat ___ (a/an/the/–) meat. I've been ___ (a/an/the/–) vegetarian for five years.
exercises.exp: meat nói chung → không dùng; nghề nghiệp/đặc điểm → a vegetarian

exercises.q: ___ (a/an/the/–) sun rises in ___ (a/an/the/–) east and sets in ___ (a/an/the/–) west.
exercises.exp: The sun (duy nhất, đã biết); the east/west (hướng cố định → dùng the)

exercises.q: We need to go to ___ (a/an/the/–) supermarket. We've run out of ___ (a/an/the/–) milk.
exercises.exp: the supermarket (đã biết cụ thể); milk nói chung → không dùng

exercises.q: It's ___ (a/an/the/–) hour's drive from here to ___ (a/an/the/–) coast.
exercises.exp: an hour (h câm, phát âm nguyên âm); the coast (cụ thể, đã biết)

exercises.q: ___ (a/an/the/–) French is spoken in many countries around the world.
exercises.exp: Ngôn ngữ → không dùng mạo từ

exercises.q: She was ___ (a/an/the/–) only student who passed ___ (a/an/the/–) exam.
exercises.exp: the only (duy nhất → the); the exam (cụ thể, đã biết)

exercises.q: What ___ (a/an/the/–) beautiful day! Let's go for ___ (a/an/the/–) walk.
exercises.exp: What a + danh từ số ít; go for a walk (lần đầu đề cập)

exercises.q: He earns about €3,000 ___ (a/an/the/–) month and works 40 hours ___ (a/an/the/–) week.
exercises.exp: Tỉ lệ → a month, a week

exercises.q: ___ (a/an/the/–) Amazon flows through ___ (a/an/the/–) Brazil.
exercises.exp: The Amazon (tên sông); Brazil (tên quốc gia) → không dùng mạo từ

exercises.q: I had ___ (a/an/the/–) interesting conversation with ___ (a/an/the/–) manager this morning.
exercises.exp: an + vowel sound; the manager (cụ thể, đã biết trong ngữ cảnh)

exercises.q: She bought ___ (a/an/the/–) house last year. ___ (a/an/the/–) house has ___ (a/an/the/–) lovely garden.
exercises.exp: Lần đầu → a; lần hai → The; lần đầu đề cập garden → a

reorder.words: She is an architect who works for a small company
The Amazon is the longest river in South America
I go to the gym three times a week
Children need love and fresh air to stay healthy
She plays the piano and he plays the guitar
What a beautiful sunset over the sea tonight
He is in hospital after a serious accident last week
The sun rises in the east every morning
I had an interesting conversation with the manager
She earns five hundred euros a month working part time
This is the best film I have seen this year
My brother is a university student studying medicine
We need to go to the supermarket to buy some milk
She was the only person who knew the answer
I love music but I cannot play a musical instrument
They had dinner at a lovely restaurant by the river
It is an hour by train from here to the capital city
French and Spanish are spoken in many countries worldwide
He left home at seven and arrived at the office at nine
The Pacific Ocean is the largest ocean in the world

Every morning she goes for {{6}} run in {{7}} park near her house. {{8}} park has {{9}} beautiful lake where she sometimes stops to watch {{10}} ducks. Last year, she visited {{11}} United States for the first time. She said it was such {{12}} amazing experience. She stayed in {{13}} hotel near {{14}} Central Park in New York and went to see {{15}} play on Broadway.

Tom usually goes to {{6}} work by bicycle, which takes {{7}} hour. He earns about €4,000 {{8}} month. After {{9}} work, he often goes to {{10}} gym or meets friends at {{11}} café near his flat. Last week he bought {{12}} new guitar. {{13}} guitar is {{14}} acoustic model made in {{15}} Spain.

{{1}} English is now considered {{2}} global language. {{3}} people who speak it as {{4}} first language number around 400 million, but {{5}} number of second-language speakers is much larger. {{6}} history of English is fascinating — it began as {{7}} Germanic language and absorbed {{8}} enormous amount of vocabulary from {{9}} French and {{10}} Latin after {{11}} Norman Conquest. Today, {{12}} English spoken in {{13}} United Kingdom differs from {{14}} English spoken in {{15}} United States in many interesting ways.

I went to {{1}} doctor last Monday because I had {{2}} terrible headache. {{3}} doctor told me I needed to rest. She wrote {{4}} prescription and told me to go to {{5}} pharmacy. {{6}} pharmacy was closed, so I had to walk to {{7}} other one on {{8}} other side of town. When I finally got home, I was exhausted. I went straight to {{9}} bed and slept for {{10}} hour. My flatmate came home later and made {{11}} soup. It was such {{12}} kind thing to do. {{13}} next morning I felt much better and was able to go to {{14}} work.

Last summer I took {{1}} trip to {{2}} Mediterranean. I visited {{3}} Greece and {{4}} Turkey in the same holiday. It was such {{5}} unforgettable experience. In {{6}} Athens, I went to see {{7}} Acropolis, which is {{8}} most famous ancient monument in {{9}} world. I also found {{10}} lovely small restaurant near {{11}} hotel where I was staying. {{12}} food was incredible. I had {{13}} glass of local wine and {{14}} fish dish that the waiter recommended. {{15}} waiter told me it was a traditional recipe.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả ngôi nhà hoặc căn phòng lý tưởng của bạn, sử dụng đúng mạo từ a/an/the và zero article
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Articles: a/an (first mention), the (second mention/specific), zero article (general)
writing_prompts.example: My ideal home would be a large house in the countryside, not too far from a small town. The house would have an open fireplace in the living room, which would make it feel warm and cosy. I would love a big kitchen with wooden floors and the kind of light that fills the room in the morning. There would be a garden at the back with an old apple tree. The garden would be the perfect place to relax on summer evenings. It would be such a peaceful place to live.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một chuyến đi bạn đã thực hiện đến một thành phố nổi tiếng, dùng đúng mạo từ khi nhắc đến địa điểm, danh lam thắng cảnh
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: The with unique/known places; a/an first mention; zero article with countries and cities
writing_prompts.example: Last spring I visited Paris for the first time. It was such an incredible experience. I stayed in a small hotel near the centre of the city. On the first day, I went to see the Eiffel Tower, which was even more impressive in real life than in photographs. I also visited the Louvre and spent an entire afternoon looking at the paintings. In the evenings, I liked to sit in a small café and watch people walk by. The food was absolutely amazing — I had the best croissant I have ever tasted.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một người nổi tiếng hoặc người bạn ngưỡng mộ – nghề nghiệp, tài năng và thành tựu của họ
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: a/an with jobs; the with unique roles/superlatives; zero article with names and abstract nouns
writing_prompts.example: The person I admire most is Nelson Mandela. He was a lawyer and a politician who became the first democratically elected president of South Africa. He spent 27 years in prison because of his beliefs, but he never gave up hope. After his release, he showed the world what forgiveness and courage truly mean. He played a key role in ending apartheid. He was such an inspiring leader that his story continues to influence people around the world. The impact he had on the idea of justice and equality is truly extraordinary.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về thói quen ăn uống của bạn – những gì bạn thích và không thích, và tại sao
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Zero article with meals and general foods; a/an first mention; the for specific items
writing_prompts.example: I try to follow a healthy diet most of the time. I don't eat meat, so I get protein from eggs, beans, and fish. Every morning I have breakfast before leaving home — usually a bowl of oats with fruit and a cup of tea. I don't drink coffee because it makes me feel anxious. At lunch I often have a salad or a sandwich. The kind of food I enjoy most is Japanese cuisine. I think sushi is such a delicious and healthy option. In the evenings I cook dinner at home, which I find relaxing.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả một buổi sáng điển hình của bạn từ lúc thức dậy đến khi đến nơi làm việc hoặc trường học
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Zero article with home/work/school/bed; a/an for first mentions; the for known/specific things
writing_prompts.example: My typical morning starts when the alarm goes off at six thirty. I get out of bed, go to the bathroom, and have a quick shower. Then I go to the kitchen and make a cup of tea and some toast. I usually spend about an hour getting ready. I leave home at seven thirty and take the bus to work. The bus is often crowded, so I prefer to listen to music through my headphones during the journey. I arrive at the office at eight fifteen. It is always such a busy morning, with emails to answer and meetings to prepare for.

intermediate.title: Reflexive pronouns: Myself, yourself
intermediate.content:
📌 Reflexive Pronouns – Bảng đại từ phản thân
SubjectReflexiveVí dụ
ImyselfI hurt myself.
you (sg)yourselfDid you enjoy yourself?
hehimselfHe cut himself shaving.
sheherselfShe taught herself Spanish.
ititselfThe door opened by itself.
weourselvesWe enjoyed ourselves.
you (pl)yourselvesHelp yourselves to food!
theythemselvesThey introduced themselves.
📌 Dùng khi nào?
1. Chủ ngữ và tân ngữ là cùng một người (reflexive use):
She cut herself chopping the onions. / We really enjoyed ourselves at the party.

2. Sau giới từ (khi chỉ về chủ ngữ):
He should be proud of himself. / She was talking to herself.
⚠️ Không dùng reflexive sau giới từ chỉ vị trí → dùng object pronoun:
❌ She left her bag in front of herself. → ✅ She left her bag in front of her.

3. Nhấn mạnh (emphatic use) – tự làm, không nhờ ai:
I will talk to Anna myself. / Paul himself designed everything.

4. By myself / by yourself… = một mình / tự làm:
She raised four children by herself. / I was on my own all morning.
⚠️ Các động từ KHÔNG dùng reflexive trong tiếng Anh
Một số động từ dùng reflexive trong tiếng Việt/tiếng khác nhưng KHÔNG dùng reflexive trong tiếng Anh:

Sai (dùng reflexive)Đúng
❌ I washed myself. (= tắm/rửa mặt)✅ I washed. / I had a shower.
❌ She dressed herself.✅ She got dressed. / She dressed.
❌ He relaxed himself.✅ He relaxed.
❌ They hurried themselves.✅ They hurried.

✅ Nhưng dùng được khi muốn nhấn mạnh: I'll do it myself!
📐 Reflexive vs Each other
• Reflexive: mỗi người tự làm với chính mình
→ They're buying themselves a new TV. (mỗi người mua cho bản thân)
• Each other: hai hoặc nhiều người làm lẫn nhau
→ They're buying each other gifts. (A mua quà cho B, B mua quà cho A)

exercises.q: She cut ___ while she was slicing vegetables.
exercises.exp: She → herself (phản thân)

exercises.q: I made this cake ___. I'm quite proud of it.
exercises.exp: I → myself (nhấn mạnh: tự làm)

exercises.q: The children are old enough to dress ___.
exercises.exp: The children (they) → themselves

exercises.q: Did you enjoy ___ at the party last night?
exercises.exp: you (singular) → yourself

exercises.q: He hurt ___ playing football on Sunday afternoon.
exercises.exp: He → himself

exercises.q: We painted the whole house ___. It took two weeks!
exercises.exp: We → ourselves (nhấn mạnh: tự làm không thuê ai)

exercises.q: The cat licked ___ clean after eating its dinner.
exercises.exp: The cat (it) → itself

exercises.q: She should be proud of ___. She worked really hard for this.
exercises.exp: She → herself (sau giới từ of)

exercises.q: He was talking to ___ when I walked into the room.
exercises.exp: He → himself (sau giới từ to, chỉ về chủ ngữ)

exercises.q: Help ___ to some more cake — there's plenty left!
exercises.exp: Nói với nhiều người (you plural) → yourselves

exercises.q: The manager ___ told me about the promotion. I was so surprised.
exercises.exp: The manager (he) → himself (nhấn mạnh: chính ông ấy nói)

exercises.q: They introduced ___ to the new team when they arrived.
exercises.exp: They → themselves (mỗi người tự giới thiệu bản thân)

exercises.q: I taught ___ to play the guitar using online videos.
exercises.exp: I → myself (tự học)

exercises.q: She lives by ___. She doesn't have any flatmates.
exercises.exp: She → herself; by herself = một mình

exercises.q: The company launched ___ as a premium brand three years ago.
exercises.exp: The company (it) → itself

exercises.q: They looked at ___ in the mirror before going on stage.
exercises.exp: themselves = mỗi người nhìn bản thân mình trong gương

exercises.q: We really enjoyed ___ on our holiday in Italy.
exercises.exp: We → ourselves

exercises.q: He fixed the car by ___ without any help from a mechanic.
exercises.exp: He → himself; by himself = tự mình, không có ai giúp

exercises.q: Please make ___ comfortable while I get you a drink.
exercises.exp: you (singular) → yourself

exercises.q: The door opened by ___ when we approached it.
exercises.exp: The door (it) → itself; by itself = tự động

exercises.q: She prepared ___ (reflexive) for the important job interview.
exercises.exp: She → herself

exercises.q: I'm going to do this project ___. I don't need any help.
exercises.exp: I → myself (nhấn mạnh: tự làm)

exercises.q: He found ___ thinking about the problem even in the middle of the night.
exercises.exp: He → himself (reflexive sau found)

exercises.q: They bought ___ a new television for their living room.
exercises.exp: They → themselves

exercises.q: Did you hurt ___ when you fell off the bicycle?
exercises.exp: you → yourself

exercises.q: The children made ___ sandwiches for the school trip.
exercises.exp: The children (they) → themselves

exercises.q: We introduced ___ to the new neighbours this morning.
exercises.exp: We → ourselves

exercises.q: She raised her three children by ___ after her husband passed away.
exercises.exp: She → herself; by herself = một mình

exercises.q: The Queen ___ presented the awards at the ceremony.
exercises.exp: The Queen (she) → herself (nhấn mạnh)

exercises.q: I cut ___ while I was opening the package with a knife.
exercises.exp: I → myself

exercises.q: Be careful! Don't burn ___ on the hot pan.
exercises.exp: you (singular) → yourself

exercises.q: The machine will restart ___ if there is a power cut.
exercises.exp: The machine (it) → itself

exercises.q: He taught ___ to code using free online tutorials.
exercises.exp: He → himself (tự học)

exercises.q: Enjoy ___, everyone! The party starts in five minutes.
exercises.exp: everyone (you plural) → yourselves

exercises.q: She was sitting by ___ at the back of the café, reading a book.
exercises.exp: She → herself; by herself = một mình

exercises.q: We organised the whole event ___. We didn't hire an event manager.
exercises.exp: We → ourselves (nhấn mạnh)

exercises.q: The cat washed ___ carefully after its meal.
exercises.exp: The cat (it) → itself

exercises.q: He was proud of ___ for finishing the marathon despite the rain.
exercises.exp: He → himself (sau giới từ of)

exercises.q: They need to believe in ___ more if they want to succeed.
exercises.exp: They → themselves (sau giới từ in)

exercises.q: I'll speak to the client ___. You don't need to come.
exercises.exp: I → myself (nhấn mạnh: chính tôi sẽ nói chuyện)

reorder.words: She taught herself to speak Spanish in just one year
I hurt myself when I fell off my bike yesterday
He fixed the computer himself without calling the technician
We really enjoyed ourselves at the concert last Saturday
The children prepared themselves for the school performance
Did you enjoy yourself at the party on Friday night
She introduced herself to the new colleagues at the meeting
He should be proud of himself for everything he has achieved
The cat washed itself carefully after finishing its dinner
They bought themselves new laptops for the university course
I will deal with this problem myself so do not worry
She lives by herself in a small flat near the city centre
Help yourselves to some food there is plenty for everyone

The door opened by itself which was very strange indeed

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một lần bạn tự mình làm điều gì đó mà không cần sự giúp đỡ của ai — có thể là học kỹ năng mới, sửa chữa đồ vật, hay tổ chức một sự kiện
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Reflexive pronouns: myself, by myself, taught myself, found myself, proud of myself
writing_prompts.example: Last year, I decided to teach myself to play the guitar. I had always wanted to play, but I never had the money for lessons, so I bought a second-hand guitar and found free tutorials online. I found myself practising for hours every evening. At first, I cut myself on the strings several times because my fingers weren't strong enough. I often felt frustrated and had to remind myself that learning takes time. After six months of practising entirely by myself, I was able to play three or four songs properly. I surprised myself with how much I had improved. When I played for my family, they could not believe I had done it all by myself. It was one of the moments I have felt proudest of myself.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một nhân vật trong phim hoặc sách mà bạn yêu thích — mô tả cách họ tự vượt qua thử thách và phát triển bản thân
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Reflexive pronouns: himself/herself, by himself/herself, taught himself/herself, proud of himself/herself
writing_prompts.example: One of my favourite characters is Hermione Granger from the Harry Potter series. She is a brilliant student who constantly challenges herself to learn more than is required. Throughout the stories, she finds herself in dangerous situations, but she always manages to stay calm and rely on herself to find a solution. She taught herself many advanced spells by reading books entirely by herself. At times, other characters underestimate her, but she always proves herself capable. I admire her because she is never afraid to stand up for herself and for what she believes is right. She is a character who clearly believes in herself, and that confidence is what makes her so inspiring.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả một buổi tối ở nhà một mình — bạn làm gì, cảm thấy thế nào, và những điều bạn tự làm được
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: By myself/yourself, found myself, enjoyed myself, kept myself busy, by herself/himself
writing_prompts.example: Last Friday, my flatmate went away for the weekend and I had the whole flat to myself. I enjoyed myself more than I expected. I cooked a proper meal for myself — pasta with a home-made sauce — and ate it while watching a film I had chosen entirely by myself. I found myself relaxing properly for the first time in weeks. Later in the evening, I ran a bath and treated myself to a long soak with candles and music. I told myself this was exactly what I needed. I kept myself busy tidying the kitchen afterwards, which was surprisingly satisfying. By the time I went to bed, I felt completely refreshed. Sometimes being by yourself is the best thing you can do.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một sự kiện gia đình hoặc bữa tiệc mà mọi người cùng nhau tổ chức — ai đã tự làm những gì
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Reflexive pronouns with various subjects: himself, herself, themselves, ourselves, yourselves
writing_prompts.example: Last month, we organised a surprise birthday party for my mother entirely by ourselves. My father cooked a three-course meal himself, which was impressive because he rarely cooks. My sister made the birthday cake herself from a recipe she had found online. The guests introduced themselves to each other warmly and helped themselves to drinks as they arrived. My grandmother, who is in her eighties, dressed herself beautifully and told everyone she had chosen her outfit herself. My mother was completely shocked when she walked in. She said she had never felt so loved. We all enjoyed ourselves tremendously. At the end of the evening, my mother said to my father: 'You should be proud of yourself — this was the best birthday I have ever had.'

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một người bạn ngưỡng mộ vì họ tự xây dựng thành công của mình mà không nhờ vào sự giúp đỡ của người khác
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Reflexive pronouns for emphasis and reflexive use: himself/herself, by himself/herself, himself/herself + emphasis
writing_prompts.example: The person I admire most is my uncle, who built his business entirely by himself starting from nothing. He taught himself accounting and marketing from books and free online courses. He told me that nobody helped him at the beginning — he found himself working twelve-hour days and often had to motivate himself when things went wrong. He never borrowed money from family and financed everything himself. What I admire most is that he believed in himself even when others doubted him. He himself once said: 'If you do not believe in yourself, nobody else will.' Today his company employs thirty people, and he is proud of himself not just for the success, but for proving that hard work and self-belief are enough.

intermediate.title: Much, many, a lot, little, few, some, any, no: Quantifiers
intermediate.content:
📌 Much / Many
Dùng trong câu phủ định và câu hỏi. Ít dùng trong câu khẳng định.
• many + danh từ đếm được số nhiều: There aren't many students here. How many eggs?
• much + danh từ không đếm được: There isn't much coffee. How much time?

✦ Dùng được trong câu khẳng định với: too much / too many và so much / so many:
There's too much salt. You eat too many biscuits. There were so many people!
📌 A lot of / Lots of / Plenty of
Dùng với cả danh từ đếm được và không đếm được. Thường dùng trong câu khẳng định.
She spends a lot of time online. We had lots of fun. There's plenty of food.
⚠️ Không dùng 'a lot of' trước tính từ/động từ — dùng a lot: I like her a lot. I don't want any more cake — I've had plenty.
📌 (A) few / (A) little
• (a) few + danh từ đếm được số nhiều: I have a few friends. Few people know this.
• (a) little + danh từ không đếm được: Add a little salt. He has little patience.

Với a (tích cực)Không có a (tiêu cực)
A few people came. (= some, enough)Few people came. (= almost none)
'Yes, I speak a little English.' (positive)'No, I speak little English.' (negative)
📌 Some / Any / No
• some: câu khẳng định + offers/requests: I bought some apples. Would you like some tea?
• any: câu phủ định và câu hỏi: I don't have any money. Do you have any questions?
• no: câu khẳng định = zero quantity: There's no milk left. She has no time.
⚠️ no đã mang nghĩa phủ định — KHÔNG dùng thêm not: ❌ I don't have no money.
📐 Bảng tổng hợp nhanh
QuantifierCountableUncountableCâu...
many✓✗Phủ định/hỏi
much✗✓Phủ định/hỏi
a lot of✓✓Khẳng định
(a) few✓✗Mọi câu
(a) little✗✓Mọi câu
some✓✓Khẳng định/offers
any✓✓Phủ định/hỏi
no✓✓Khẳng định (= zero)

exercises.q: There isn't ___ milk left. Can you buy some?
exercises.exp: Câu phủ định + uncountable → any

exercises.q: She has ___ friends in the new city, so she feels lonely.
exercises.exp: few (không có 'a') = gần như không có → tiêu cực

exercises.q: Would you like ___ more tea? There's plenty in the pot.
exercises.exp: Offer/request → some

exercises.q: How ___ time do we have before the train leaves?
exercises.exp: How much + uncountable noun (time)

exercises.q: There's ___ traffic today — it's taking forever to get home.
exercises.exp: Câu khẳng định, nhấn mạnh → so much + uncountable

exercises.q: 'Shall I buy some bread?' 'No need — there are ___ slices left.'
exercises.exp: a few = some, enough → tích cực

exercises.q: He spoke ___ English when he arrived, but he's improving fast.
exercises.exp: little (không có 'a') + uncountable → tiêu cực (gần như không biết)

exercises.q: Do you have ___ questions before we start?
exercises.exp: Câu hỏi → any

exercises.q: There were ___ people at the concert — the whole stadium was full!
exercises.exp: Câu khẳng định nhấn mạnh + countable → so many

exercises.q: I don't have ___ money with me. Can I pay by card?
exercises.exp: Câu phủ định + uncountable → much

exercises.q: She spends ___ time exercising — at least two hours a day.
exercises.exp: Câu khẳng định + uncountable → a lot of

exercises.q: There's ___ point in arguing — he never changes his mind.
exercises.exp: no = zero + câu khẳng định; no point = không có ích gì

exercises.q: 'Is there any coffee?' 'Yes, there's ___ left in the pot.'
exercises.exp: a little + uncountable → tích cực (có đủ)

exercises.q: How ___ siblings do you have?
exercises.exp: How many + countable noun (siblings)

exercises.q: She has ___ experience in marketing, which is why they hired her.
exercises.exp: Câu khẳng định + uncountable → a lot of

exercises.q: There are ___ seats left — you'd better book quickly.
exercises.exp: few (không có 'a') = gần như không còn → tiêu cực, cấp bách

exercises.q: I bought ___ souvenirs to bring back for my family.
exercises.exp: Câu khẳng định → some

exercises.q: He doesn't speak ___ Japanese, but he knows a few phrases.
exercises.exp: Câu phủ định + uncountable → much

exercises.q: There are too ___ cars on the road these days.
exercises.exp: too many + countable → too many cars

exercises.q: We have ___ time to spare, so let's have a coffee before we leave.
exercises.exp: a little = some time, enough → tích cực

exercises.q: I don't have ___ (many/much) patience for people who are always late.
exercises.exp: patience = uncountable → much

exercises.q: There are ___ (few/a few) good restaurants in this area — you'll love it.
exercises.exp: a few = some, positive → có vài nhà hàng tốt

exercises.q: She earns ___ (many/a lot of) money in her new job.
exercises.exp: Câu khẳng định + uncountable → a lot of

exercises.q: We don't have ___ (some/any) eggs left. I need to go shopping.
exercises.exp: Câu phủ định → any

exercises.q: There was ___ (few/little) evidence to support the theory.
exercises.exp: little + uncountable → tiêu cực (gần như không có bằng chứng)

exercises.q: How ___ (many/much) information do you need to complete the report?
exercises.exp: information = uncountable → how much

exercises.q: I have ___ (so many/so much) work to do that I don't know where to start.
exercises.exp: work = uncountable → so much

exercises.q: 'Would you like ___ (some/any) cake?' 'Yes please, just a small piece.'
exercises.exp: Offer → some

exercises.q: There are too ___ (much/many) mistakes in this essay. Please revise it.
exercises.exp: mistakes = countable → too many

exercises.q: There's ___ (no/any) need to shout — I can hear you perfectly well.
exercises.exp: no + noun in affirmative = zero quantity

exercises.q: ___ (Few/A few) students understood the explanation on the first try.
exercises.exp: Few (no 'a') = almost none → negative meaning

exercises.q: She spent ___ (a little/little) time reading each evening before bed.
exercises.exp: a little = some, positive → tích cực

exercises.q: They didn't have ___ (some/any) problems on the journey.
exercises.exp: Câu phủ định → any

exercises.q: There were ___ (so many/so much) people at the festival that we couldn't move.
exercises.exp: people = countable → so many

exercises.q: He has ___ (few/a few) ideas about what to do this weekend — want to join?
exercises.exp: a few = some ideas → positive (đủ để làm gì đó)

exercises.q: I don't drink ___ (many/much) coffee anymore. Just one cup a day.
exercises.exp: coffee = uncountable → much

exercises.q: There's ___ (some/any) interesting news in today's paper.
exercises.exp: Câu khẳng định → some

exercises.q: ___ (Few/A few) people know the truth about what really happened.
exercises.exp: Few (không có 'a') = almost nobody knows → negative

exercises.q: She has ___ (many/a lot of) experience with children, having worked as a nanny.
exercises.exp: experience = uncountable; câu khẳng định → a lot of

exercises.q: There's ___ (no/any) sugar in this coffee and it tastes terrible.
exercises.exp: no = zero sugar; câu khẳng định

There are {{3}} things to do — museums, restaurants, cinemas and parks — but there is also {{4}} noise and {{5}} traffic. {{6}} people love the energy of city life, but {{7}} prefer the quiet of the countryside. writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả thói quen ăn uống hàng ngày của bạn — dùng các quantifiers để nói về lượng thức ăn và đồ uống
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Quantifiers: a lot of, some, a few, a little, much, many, no, any, too much/many
writing_prompts.example: I try to eat a balanced diet every day. I drink a lot of water — at least two litres — and I avoid too much sugar and processed food. For breakfast I usually have some fruit and a little yoghurt, which gives me plenty of energy for the morning. I don't eat much red meat, but I cook a few fish dishes each week. I have no strong feelings about vegetarian food — I just try to make sure I get enough protein. For snacks, I have a few nuts or some crackers. I don't drink many fizzy drinks because I think there is too much sugar in them. Overall, I feel that a few simple changes to your diet can make a big difference to how you feel.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một chuyến mua sắm — những gì bạn cần mua, có bao nhiêu thứ, và kết quả chuyến đi
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Quantifiers in shopping context: a lot of, some, a few, no, any, much, many, plenty of
writing_prompts.example: Last Saturday I went to the supermarket to do my weekly shopping. I had a long list because I hadn't bought much food during the week. I needed a lot of fruit and vegetables, some bread, and a few tins of beans. When I arrived at the supermarket, there were so many people that I had to wait a few minutes to get a trolley. There was no fresh pasta left, which was disappointing, but there was plenty of dried pasta. I found a little time to browse the reduced section and found some good deals. I didn't spend too much money in the end — just a little over my usual budget. By the time I got home, I had enough food for the whole week.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một vấn đề trong khu phố hoặc thành phố nơi bạn sống — dùng quantifiers để mô tả quy mô vấn đề
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Quantifiers for problems and quantities: too much, too many, few, little, no, some, a lot of
writing_prompts.example: One of the biggest problems in my neighbourhood is traffic. There are too many cars on the roads at rush hour, and there is so much pollution that it affects people's health. There are a few cycle paths, but they are not long enough to be useful. Very little has been done to improve public transport in recent years — there are few reliable bus routes and no metro system. There is a lot of frustration among residents, but little official action. Some residents have written letters to the local council, but there has been no response yet. I think a few simple changes — such as creating more green spaces and closing some roads to traffic — could make a big difference.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một khoá học hay hoạt động mới bạn gần đây bắt đầu — dùng quantifiers để mô tả tiến độ và khó khăn
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Quantifiers: a lot of, a few, a little, not much, some, no, many, few
writing_prompts.example: A few months ago I started learning photography as a hobby. At first I knew very little about the technical side, and there was a lot of new information to absorb. I watched some online tutorials and read a few books on the subject. I didn't have much money for equipment, so I started with a second-hand camera. Some of the techniques were quite difficult to master, and there were many days when I felt frustrated. However, I made a little progress each week. After a few months, I could take photos that I was genuinely proud of. There is no substitute for practice — the more photos you take, the better you become. I still have a lot to learn, but I have plenty of enthusiasm.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả một bữa tiệc hoặc sự kiện mà bạn đã tổ chức hay tham dự — dùng quantifiers để nói về số người, thức ăn, đồ uống
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Quantifiers for events: a lot of, many, some, a few, plenty of, no, too many, not much
writing_prompts.example: Last month I organised a birthday party for my best friend. We invited about thirty people, so there was a lot of preparation to do. I cooked some dishes myself and asked a few friends to bring something too. There was plenty of food — in fact, there was too much, and we had a lot of leftovers. I bought some balloons and a few decorations, which made the room look festive. There were so many people that the flat felt quite cramped, but everyone seemed to enjoy themselves. There was no need to worry about music because a friend brought a speaker. We had very little trouble with the neighbours — they were very understanding. Overall, it was a great evening and I received a lot of lovely messages the next day.

intermediate.title: All, both: Quantifiers
intermediate.content:
📌 ALL – Dùng khi nào?
1. All + noun (chung chung — không có the):
All plants need water. All music makes people feel something.

2. All + the/my/Tom's... + noun (cụ thể):
All the students in my class passed. All my friends came.

3. All (of) + the/my... + noun: (of có thể bỏ)
All (of) the students passed. All (of) the music I download makes me feel good.

4. All of + us/you/them (object pronoun — bắt buộc dùng of):
All of us were at the party. She invited all of us.
❌ All us were at the party.

5. Vị trí giữa câu (mid position):
We all went. / They were all happy. / We can all be there.
(Trước động từ chính; sau be; sau auxiliary đầu tiên)

6. All + biểu thức thời gian = suốt cả:
I studied all day and all night. We'll be here all week.
❗ Không dùng mạo từ hay giới từ: ❌ all the day / for all day
📌 BOTH – Dùng khi nào?
Luôn nói về 2 người/vật cụ thể.

1. Both + noun HOẶC Both the/my... + noun:
Both students passed. = Both the students passed.

2. Both (of) + the/my... + noun: (of có thể bỏ)
Both (of) your parents are kind.

3. Both of + us/you/them (bắt buộc dùng of):
Both of them did well. / Can both of you come?
❌ Both them did well.

4. Vị trí giữa câu (mid position):
We both like cinema. / They were both wrong.

5. Dùng sau tân ngữ (object position):
She loves them both. / I know them both.
📐 So sánh ALL vs BOTH
ALLBOTH
3+ người/vật, hoặc khái niệm chungChính xác 2 người/vật
All students hate homework.Both students passed.
All of us went.Both of them went.
We all agreed.We both agreed.

passage.text: My two sisters and I are very close. {{1}} three of us grew up in the same small town and went to {{2}} the same school. My sisters are twins, and {{3}} have always been very competitive with each other. {{4}} of them are doctors now, which our parents are incredibly proud of. I sometimes feel that {{5}} the attention in our family goes to them! But I am not jealous — I admire them {{6}}. We {{7}} have different interests outside of work. I love cooking and spend {{8}} weekend trying new recipes. My sister Anna enjoys photography, and she has used {{9}} her holiday time this year taking pictures abroad. My other sister, Mia, practises yoga {{10}} day without fail. {{11}} of us speak a little French, which is useful when we travel together.

The two candidates for the position were very impressive. {{1}} had excellent qualifications and {{2}} had years of relevant experience. The interview panel — {{3}} five of us — agreed that the decision would be difficult. We {{4}} had different opinions about which candidate was stronger. One panellist felt that {{5}} candidates deserved the role equally. In the end, we spent {{6}} morning debating. We were {{7}} a little tired by the afternoon session. {{8}} of the candidates were told that their performance had been outstanding. {{9}} will be invited back for a second interview. The process has shown us that {{10}} the hard work we put into recruiting is worthwhile.

My grandmother turned eighty last spring and {{1}} her children came to celebrate. There are five of them — {{2}} live abroad and {{3}} three live nearby. They {{4}} agreed it would be a surprise party. {{5}} the planning was done in secret over several weeks. My mother baked a cake; {{6}} the food was prepared by the family. {{7}} the guests arrived before six o'clock. My grandmother walked in and {{8}} of us shouted 'Surprise!' She laughed and said she had suspected {{9}} along. We spent {{10}} evening eating, talking, and looking at old photographs. It was {{11}} one of us had hoped for.

I have two very close friends from university — Minh and Sara. {{1}} of them were on my course in the first year. We {{2}} lived in the same halls of residence. {{3}} of them are creative — Minh paints and Sara writes poetry. I spent {{4}} last summer travelling with them. We visited four countries, and we {{5}} agreed it was the best trip of our lives. {{6}} the photographs from that trip are saved in a shared album. There were moments when {{7}} of us felt tired or frustrated, but we always managed to cheer each other up. Now we live in different cities, but we {{8}} try to meet at least twice a year. {{9}} of the memories we made together are ones I treasure. I feel lucky to have {{10}} of them in my life.

Our company held an annual review last month. {{1}} departments were asked to present their results. Some presentations were brief; {{2}} took longer than expected. The director listened carefully to {{3}} of them. She took notes {{4}} morning. At the end, she said that {{5}} teams had worked hard and that the results were largely positive. {{6}} of the targets had been met, which was encouraging. Two departments had exceeded expectations; {{11}} had fallen slightly short. The director said she was proud of {{7}} the staff and thanked {{8}} of us individually. She also announced that {{9}} employees would receive a bonus. We {{10}} left the meeting feeling motivated.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về hai người bạn thân của bạn — họ giống và khác nhau như thế nào, và những gì bạn cùng nhau chia sẻ
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Both (of them), both… and…, all three of us, we all, they both
writing_prompts.example: My two closest friends are Linh and Minh. Both of them have known me since primary school, and both have been there for me during some of the most important moments in my life. They both love music, but they have completely different tastes — Linh prefers classical while Minh is a big fan of hip-hop. Both are funny and kind, and both have a very different sense of humour, which I find charming. All three of us meet up at least once a month. We all enjoy trying new restaurants together. I am really lucky to have both of them in my life.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả một ngày bạn làm việc hoặc học tập rất chăm chỉ — dùng 'all day', 'all morning', 'all night', v.v.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: All + time expression: all day, all morning, all night, all week, all weekend
writing_prompts.example: The week before my final exams was the most intense of my life. I studied all day and all night on Monday and Tuesday. I barely ate — I just drank coffee and stayed at my desk all morning and all afternoon. By Wednesday I had been awake for almost all night without proper sleep and I was exhausted. I worked all weekend too, barely leaving the flat. All the effort I put in during those five days was enormous. By the time the exams arrived, I felt prepared but drained. I slept all day on Saturday after it was over.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một nhóm bạn bè hoặc đồng nghiệp — những gì tất cả mọi người chia sẻ hoặc làm cùng nhau
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: All of us, we all, all my, all the (specific), all + time
writing_prompts.example: My team at work is made up of six people from four different countries. All of us joined the company within the same year, which helped us bond quickly. We all share the same passion for the project we are working on. All my colleagues are talented and dedicated — I feel lucky to work with them. Last month we had a deadline and we all worked late every evening. We spent all week preparing the final presentation. All the effort was worth it when the client told us it was excellent. We all went out for dinner afterwards to celebrate.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một chuyến đi nhóm bạn bè — những gì tất cả mọi người làm cùng nhau và những khoảnh khắc cả hai (nếu chỉ 2 người) hoặc tất cả đặc biệt chia sẻ
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: All of us, we all, both (for 2 people), they both, all the, all + time
writing_prompts.example: Last summer I went on a road trip with five friends. All of us had been planning the trip for months. We all agreed that the destination should be somewhere none of us had visited before, so we chose the Scottish Highlands. We drove all day on the first day, stopping at viewpoints and taking photographs. All the scenery was spectacular — it was unlike anything I had seen before. By evening we had all arrived at the cottage we had rented for the week. All of us cooked dinner together that first night, which was chaotic but fun. My two closest friends — Selin and Tom — had known each other longest. They both said it was the best trip they had ever taken. All of us agreed that we would do it again next year.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về hai người bạn thân nhất của bạn — những gì cả hai cùng chia sẻ và những gì tất cả bạn bè trong nhóm thích làm cùng nhau
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Both, both of them, we all, all three of us, they both, all my
writing_prompts.example: My two best friends are Hana and Daniel. Both of them have been part of my life since university. They both studied different subjects — Hana studied architecture and Daniel studied economics — but all three of us were in the same halls of residence in our first year. Both are creative in very different ways. Hana designs beautiful spaces; Daniel writes music in his spare time. We all share a love of good food and we try to cook together at least once a month. Both of them are honest with me, which I value enormously. All my favourite memories from the last decade involve one or both of them. We all hope to travel together again soon — somewhere new, somewhere we can all discover together.

intermediate.title: Both, either, neither: Quantifiers
intermediate.content:
📌 BOTH / EITHER / NEITHER – Tóm tắt nhanh
Cả ba từ này đều dùng để nói về đúng 2 người hoặc 2 vật.

TừNghĩaVí dụ
bothcả hai (= A và B)I like both cars.
eithermột trong hai (= A hoặc B)You can park on either side.
neitherkhông cái nào trong hai (= không A cũng không B)Neither parent was there.

🔍 Lưu ý ngữ pháp:
• both + plural noun: both cars, both sisters
• either / neither + singular noun: either side, neither candidate
📌 Both (of) / Either of / Neither of + noun/pronoun
Both (of) + the/my/... + noun: (of có thể bỏ)
Both (of) your parents are kind. = Both your parents are kind.

Either of / Neither of + the/my/... + noun HOẶC + us/you/them:
Either of those two dates is fine. / Neither of the candidates won.
Can either of you help me? / Neither of us knew the answer.

⚠️ neither of → dùng động từ số ít (hoặc số nhiều nếu sau noun số nhiều):
Neither of the candidates is/are good.
📌 Both…and / Either…or / Neither…nor
• both…and… = cả A lẫn B:
She speaks both French and Spanish. / Both Susan and Peter helped me.

• either…or… = hoặc A hoặc B:
You can either wait here or go home. / Either you come now, or I leave.

• neither…nor… = không A cũng không B (dùng với động từ khẳng định):
I like neither maths nor physics. / He neither called nor texted.
❌ Neither John nor Paula can't come. → ✅ Neither John nor Paula can come.
📌 Dùng độc lập (standalone) — không theo sau noun
A: Do you speak French or Spanish? B: I speak both.
A: Do you want tea or coffee? B: Either. I don't mind.
A: Which do you prefer? B: Neither. I don't like them.

⚠️ neither = từ phủ định → chỉ dùng với động từ khẳng định:
❌ I don't like neither option. → ✅ I like neither option. / I don't like either option.

passage.text: I have two brothers, Marco and Leo. {{1}} are very different from each other, which makes family dinners interesting! Marco loves sport — he plays {{2}} football and basketball. Leo, on the other hand, enjoys reading and painting. {{3}} of them is interested in the other's hobbies. At mealtimes, they {{4}} talk a lot, but about completely different things. My mother said to me once: 'I love {{5}} of them equally.' I feel the same. When there's a decision to make, {{6}} tends to have a strong opinion — it's hard to get them to agree. But they are {{7}} kind and generous when it matters. {{8}} of them has ever let me down when I really needed help. I wouldn't trade {{9}} of them for anything. Having {{10}} of them in my life makes me happy.

We had to choose between two hotels for our anniversary trip. {{1}} seemed good on paper, and {{2}} had swimming pools and free breakfast. However, when we read the reviews more carefully, {{3}} of them had received consistently good feedback — one had complaints about noise, and the other had issues with the service. We were disappointed because {{4}} option seemed perfect. I called the hotels to ask about availability. The first one said {{5}} standard {{6}} superior rooms were available that weekend. The second hotel said {{7}} of those dates could be guaranteed. We {{8}} felt frustrated. In the end, we chose a completely different hotel that {{9}} of us had considered at first. It turned out to be the best choice — {{10}} the location and the service were excellent.

My two flatmates could not agree on where to go for dinner. One wanted Thai food; the other preferred Italian. {{1}} of them asked my opinion, which I appreciated. I said that {{2}} option sounded good to me. {{3}} of the restaurants was particularly close, so travel time was not a factor. {{4}} had been reviewed well online. We decided to try one this week and {{5}} next week. At the restaurant, we ordered several dishes to share. {{6}} of us liked the spring rolls, but {{7}} enjoyed the main course. The dessert menu had two options — one was chocolate-based and {{8}} was fruit-based. {{9}} of us chose the chocolate option. By the end of the evening, {{10}} flatmates said they were happy with the choice.

I applied for two scholarships last year. {{1}} required a personal statement and two references. {{2}} of them offered full funding, which would have been life-changing. I worked hard on {{3}} applications. My tutor read them and said {{4}} was well-written. The deadline for {{5}} fell in the same week, which was stressful. I submitted {{6}} on time. Then came the waiting. After a month, I heard back from one — I had been unsuccessful. {{7}} had responded yet. When the second reply finally arrived, it was also a rejection. {{8}} scholarship was awarded to me. I was disappointed, but my tutor reminded me that {{9}} experience had made me a stronger writer. I plan to apply again and will try {{10}} route — perhaps a different funding body entirely.

There were two candidates for the team leader position. {{1}} had strong CVs and performed well at interview. One had more technical experience; {{2}} had stronger people skills. {{3}} was obviously the perfect candidate — the decision was very difficult. The hiring panel discussed {{4}} profiles at length. Some members preferred one candidate; {{5}} favoured the other. In the end, {{6}} of the panel members could reach a unanimous decision, so they invited {{7}} candidates back for a second interview. This time, {{8}} brought new ideas that impressed the panel. One focused on efficiency; {{9}} focused on team culture. The panel felt that {{10}} approaches were valuable and decided to offer positions to both.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về hai lựa chọn bạn phải đưa ra quyết định — có thể là công việc, trường học, nơi ở hoặc kế hoạch du lịch
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Both, either, neither; both…and; either…or; neither…nor
writing_prompts.example: Last year I had to choose between two job offers. Both positions were in interesting companies and both offered a good salary. However, neither was exactly what I had imagined. The first job was in marketing, which I enjoy, but neither the location nor the commute was convenient. The second was closer to home but involved neither the type of work nor the team environment I was hoping for. In the end, I decided that either option was better than staying in my current role. I chose the first job because, although neither was perfect, both my instinct and the advice of a mentor pointed in that direction. I'm glad I made the decision.

writing_prompts.vi: So sánh hai người bạn thân hoặc hai thành viên trong gia đình — điểm giống và khác nhau
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Both, neither, either; both…and; neither…nor; both of them, neither of them
writing_prompts.example: My two aunts, Clara and Rosa, are very different people. Both are warm and generous, but neither is particularly patient. Clara is organised and loves planning, while Rosa prefers to do things spontaneously. Neither of them is shy — both love to talk. They both have a great sense of humour, though in different ways. Clara makes clever, dry jokes, while Rosa tells long funny stories. Neither of them enjoys cooking, which means family gatherings usually involve either ordering food or asking someone else to cook. I love both of them equally, and I feel lucky that both my aunt and her sister have always been there for me.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về hai bộ phim, cuốn sách, hoặc chương trình TV bạn gần đây xem hoặc đọc — nhận xét về cả hai
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Both, neither, either in reviews; both were, neither was, I liked/disliked both/neither
writing_prompts.example: I recently watched two highly recommended films in the same week. Both were set in the 1970s, and both had been praised by critics. However, I have to admit that neither completely lived up to the hype. The first was visually beautiful, but neither the story nor the characters felt particularly original. The second had a stronger plot, but either the pacing was too slow or the ending was too abrupt — I couldn't quite decide which bothered me more. That said, I liked both films overall and would recommend either to anyone who enjoys slow-burning dramas. I suppose neither was perfect, but both offered something worth watching.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả một tình huống bạn phải chọn giữa hai lựa chọn — cả hai đều có ưu và nhược điểm. Dùng both, either và neither để so sánh.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Both options, either…or, neither…nor, neither of them, both have
writing_prompts.example: Last year I had to choose between staying in my current job or accepting a new position in another city. Both options were genuinely attractive. My current job offered security and familiar colleagues, while the new role offered better pay and career development. Either choice would have been reasonable. Neither was without risk, however. My current company could not promise a promotion, and moving cities meant leaving my family and friends behind. Neither of those prospects was easy to accept. I spent several weeks thinking it over. In the end I decided that neither option was perfect, but that either would lead to growth in different ways. I chose the new job, and although I still miss home, I am glad I took the chance.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một buổi tranh luận hoặc cuộc trò chuyện giữa hai người có quan điểm đối lập — dùng both, either, neither để mô tả lập trường của từng người.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Neither agreed, both believed, either could be right, neither…nor, both…and
writing_prompts.example: My two flatmates had a long argument about whether to get a pet. One wanted a cat; the other preferred a dog. Neither was willing to compromise at first. Both made reasonable points — one argued that a cat was easier to care for, while the other insisted that a dog was more companionable. Either animal would have been a welcome addition to the flat, I thought. Neither of them asked my opinion, which I found slightly amusing. Both had lived with pets before, so both knew what was involved. In the end, neither got what they originally wanted — they agreed on a hamster instead. Both seemed happy with that outcome, and so did the hamster.

intermediate.title: Any, no, none: Quantifiers
intermediate.content:
📌 ANY
1. Câu phủ định và câu hỏi:
I don't have any money. / Do you have any questions? / Is there any milk left?

2. Câu khẳng định mang nghĩa 'bất kỳ cái nào':
You can choose any colour you like. / Come at any time — I'm always home.

3. Dùng như đại từ (không theo sau noun):
'Is there any milk?' 'No, there isn't any.'
📌 NO
1. Câu khẳng định = zero quantity (thay cho not any):
I have no money. = I don't have any money.
There's no time left. = There isn't any time left.

⚠️ no đã là phủ định → KHÔNG dùng thêm not:
❌ I don't have no money. ✅ I have no money. / I don't have any money.

2. No + noun (đếm được số ít hoặc số nhiều / không đếm được):
There are no tickets left. / She has no experience. / There's no reason to worry.
📌 NONE
None = đại từ, không theo sau bởi noun:
'How many tickets are left?' 'None.'
I asked for help but none of them could assist me.

None of + the/my/... + noun hoặc + us/you/them:
None of the students failed. / None of us knew.

Cấu trúcVí dụGhi chú
no + nounThere are no tickets.Determiner, trước noun
none (alone)'Any tickets?' 'None.'Pronoun, không có noun
none of + groupNone of the students failed.Pronoun + of

🔍 Động từ sau none of: có thể số ít hoặc số nhiều:
None of the students was/were late.
📐 So sánh nhanh ANY / NO / NONE
anynonone
Trước noun: phủ định/hỏi hoặc 'bất kỳ'Trước noun trong câu khẳng địnhĐại từ, không có noun theo sau
Don't have any time.Have no time.None of them came.

exercises.q: There isn't ___ coffee left. I need to buy some.
exercises.exp: Câu phủ định (isn't) → any

exercises.q: 'How many students passed?' '___. They all failed.'
exercises.exp: None = pronoun standalone, không theo sau noun

exercises.q: She has ___ experience in this field, which is a problem.
exercises.exp: no + noun trong câu khẳng định = zero

exercises.q: You can choose ___ seat you like — the theatre isn't full.
exercises.exp: any = bất kỳ chỗ ngồi nào (câu khẳng định, ý nghĩa tự do chọn)

exercises.q: ___ of the emails I sent last week have been answered.
exercises.exp: None of + the + noun → đại từ + of

exercises.q: There are ___ tickets left for the concert. It's sold out.
exercises.exp: no + plural noun trong câu khẳng định

exercises.q: Do you have ___ idea where she put the keys?
exercises.exp: Câu hỏi → any

exercises.q: 'Is there any bread?' 'Sorry, there's ___.'
exercises.exp: none = pronoun (thay cho 'no bread'), không theo sau noun

exercises.q: ___ of us knew the answer to that difficult question.
exercises.exp: None of + us (pronoun) → đại từ + of

exercises.q: There was ___ reason to panic, so I stayed calm.
exercises.exp: no + noun (reason) trong câu khẳng định

exercises.q: 'Did anyone call while I was out?' '___, I'm afraid.'
exercises.exp: No one = không ai (pronoun) → standalone

exercises.q: She had ___ trouble finding the office. The directions were clear.
exercises.exp: no + uncountable noun trong câu khẳng định

exercises.q: Feel free to come at ___ time that's convenient for you.
exercises.exp: any time = bất kỳ lúc nào (câu khẳng định, ý nghĩa tự do)

exercises.q: ___ of the information was useful for the report.
exercises.exp: None of + the + uncountable noun

exercises.q: I didn't understand ___ of the words in that paragraph.
exercises.exp: Câu phủ định (didn't) + any of + the + noun

exercises.q: There's ___ point arguing. He never changes his mind.
exercises.exp: no point = không có ích gì (no + uncountable noun)

exercises.q: 'How much money did you spend?' 'Almost ___. Everything was free.'
exercises.exp: none = pronoun (= no money), standalone

exercises.q: I have tried all the options and ___ of them worked.
exercises.exp: none of + them (pronoun) = không cái nào (3+)

exercises.q: ___ student in the class had read the chapter before arriving.
exercises.exp: No + singular noun trong câu khẳng định

exercises.q: You can ask ___ of the teachers for help if you need it.
exercises.exp: any of = bất kỳ ai trong số các giáo viên

exercises.q: There are ___ (no/any/none) direct trains to the airport on Sundays.
exercises.exp: no + plural noun trong câu khẳng định

exercises.q: 'Did anyone volunteer?' '___ (No/None/Any) at all. They all left.'
exercises.exp: None = pronoun standalone

exercises.q: I don't have ___ (no/any/none) plans for this evening.
exercises.exp: Câu phủ định (don't) → any

exercises.q: ___ (No/None/Any) of the students had completed the homework.
exercises.exp: None of + the + noun

exercises.q: She made ___ (no/any/none) mistakes in the entire test. Perfect score!
exercises.exp: no + noun (mistakes) trong câu khẳng định

exercises.q: Feel free to call at ___ (no/any/none) time. I'm always available.
exercises.exp: any time = bất kỳ lúc nào (câu khẳng định)

exercises.q: There is ___ (no/any/none) evidence to support that claim.
exercises.exp: no + uncountable noun trong câu khẳng định

exercises.q: 'How many cakes are left?' '___ (No/None/Any), I'm afraid. They're all gone.'
exercises.exp: None = pronoun, không theo sau noun

exercises.q: ___ (No/None/Any) of the solutions they proposed would actually work.
exercises.exp: None of + the + noun

exercises.q: He searched everywhere but could find ___ (no/any/none) of his files.
exercises.exp: find none of (pronoun) sau động từ phủ định → none of

exercises.q: There was ___ (no/any/none) water left in the bottle after our long walk.
exercises.exp: no + uncountable noun trong câu khẳng định

exercises.q: Did you receive ___ (no/any/none) feedback from the panel after the interview?
exercises.exp: Câu hỏi → any

exercises.q: 'Which of the entries won a prize?' '___ (No/None/Any) — they were all disqualified.'
exercises.exp: None = pronoun standalone (không cái nào)

exercises.q: She has ___ (no/any/none) experience in sales, but she is willing to learn.
exercises.exp: no + noun (experience) trong câu khẳng định

exercises.q: I looked everywhere but couldn't find ___ (no/any/none) of the missing documents.
exercises.exp: couldn't find any of = phủ định với any of

exercises.q: There are ___ (no/any/none) seats available on the early morning flight.
exercises.exp: no + plural noun trong câu khẳng định

exercises.q: If you have ___ (no/any/none) questions, please feel free to ask at any time.
exercises.exp: Câu điều kiện if → any

exercises.q: ___ (No/None/Any) of the explanations he gave were convincing enough.
exercises.exp: None of + the + noun (pronoun position)

exercises.q: We had ___ (no/any/none) choice but to cancel the event due to the weather.
exercises.exp: no choice = không có lựa chọn nào (cụm cố định)

exercises.q: The teacher asked whether ___ (no/any/none) of us had read the article in advance.
exercises.exp: Câu hỏi gián tiếp → any of
