passage.text: When I moved to a new city, I had {{1}} friends there and {{2}} of my colleagues lived nearby. The first few weeks were difficult because there was {{3}} I could call for company. I had {{4}} idea where anything was — {{5}} of the streets made sense to me yet. I tried to stay positive, but there were days when I had {{6}} motivation to go out. I joined {{7}} clubs or activities at first, which made things worse. Then I met a woman in my building who invited me to a community event. I went, even though I had {{8}} high expectations. It turned out to be wonderful — {{9}} of the people there were unfriendly. In fact, I made {{10}} new friends that evening. I no longer have {{11}} reason to feel lonely in this city.

The investigation found that there was {{1}} evidence of wrongdoing. {{2}} of the documents examined showed {{3}} unusual financial activity. Investigators had {{4}} leads to follow at first. {{5}} of the witnesses were willing to speak. There was {{6}} way to access the sealed records without a court order. The team had {{7}} choice but to apply for one. When the order came through, they found that {{8}} of the files had been deleted — {{9}} trace of the original transactions remained. {{10}} of the senior managers could explain the missing data. The report concluded that {{11}} further action could be taken without additional evidence. {{12}} responsible parties were ever identified.

The conference was supposed to start at nine, but there was {{1}} sign of the organisers. {{2}} of the speakers had arrived yet. There were {{3}} programmes on the chairs, and {{4}} of the microphones were working. The technician checked the equipment but could find {{5}} obvious fault. There was {{6}} water on the tables, which several delegates complained about. I asked a staff member if there would be {{7}} delay — she shook her head but said {{8}} nothing. {{9}} of us in the audience really knew what was happening. Finally, at half past nine, {{10}} explanation was given and the event began without {{11}} further problems.

I tried to book a table at three different restaurants last Saturday evening. The first said they had {{1}} availability until the following week. The second had {{2}} tables free before nine o'clock, which was too late. The third did not answer — there was {{3}} reply at all. I checked online but found {{4}} of the restaurants I wanted had last-minute slots. There was {{5}} point continuing to search on the same websites. I asked two friends for suggestions, but {{6}} of them had {{7}} useful ideas either. We decided to cook at home instead. There was {{8}} bread and {{9}} eggs left in the kitchen, but we managed to make a simple pasta dish. It was {{10}} trouble at all, and actually more enjoyable than going out.

When I arrived at the rental flat, I realised there was {{1}} key in the lockbox. I called the agency but got {{2}} answer. I waited outside for twenty minutes — there was {{3}} shelter from the rain and {{4}} nearby café I could see to wait in. {{5}} of the neighbours I knocked on seemed to be home. I sent an email but received {{6}} response for over an hour. I had {{7}} idea what to do. There was {{8}} choice but to wait. Eventually a man appeared and introduced himself as the landlord. He apologised and said there had been {{9}} miscommunication. He opened the flat and I saw that {{10}} of the furniture listed in the description was actually there. It was not a good start.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một lần bạn hoặc ai đó đến một nơi nhưng tìm thấy rằng mọi thứ đã hết hoặc không có sẵn
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: No, none, any: no tickets, no seats, none left, not any, none of them
writing_prompts.example: Last summer I travelled to the coast for a weekend break without booking in advance. When I arrived at the train station, I found that there were no taxis available and none of the hire cars were left. I had no idea the town would be so busy. I walked to three different hotels, but none of them had any rooms. There was no tourist information office open, and none of the people I asked could help. I had no option but to look online. Eventually I found a small guesthouse outside town that had one room left. There was no restaurant nearby, so I had no dinner that night. It was not the relaxing weekend I had planned, but it taught me to always book in advance.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả một tình huống khó khăn khi bạn không có đủ thứ bạn cần — dùng no, none, any
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: No + noun, none of, not any, any + noun for questions
writing_prompts.example: Last month I had a very stressful few days. I had no internet connection at home, which made working from home impossible. I checked the router — none of the lights were on. I called the provider, but none of the solutions they gave me worked. I had no way to attend my online meetings and no one could fix the problem quickly. None of my neighbours had a spare network I could use. I had no choice but to go to a café every day. By the end of the week, I had no patience left. When the connection was finally restored, I felt enormous relief. I now have a backup plan so that there is no repeat of that situation.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về điều gì đó bạn không biết gì về nó trước đây, nhưng bây giờ bạn hiểu rõ
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: No knowledge/experience/idea, none of, any understanding, not any clue
writing_prompts.example: When I started my current job, I had no experience with data analysis and no idea how to use the software the team relied on. None of the training materials made sense to me at first. I had no background in statistics and none of the terminology was familiar. I had no way of knowing how long it would take me to learn. None of my previous jobs had involved anything similar. I spent the first month feeling quite lost — there were days when I had no confidence at all. But gradually, none of my fears seemed as overwhelming. I asked questions, made no assumptions, and took things step by step. After three months, none of it seemed difficult anymore.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một sự kiện hoặc dịp mà mọi thứ đều không có sẵn — vé, thức ăn, chỗ ngồi — dùng no, none, any để diễn đạt sự vắng mặt.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: No tickets, none left, there was no, none of them, not any, there were no
writing_prompts.example: I went to the concert hall last Saturday hoping to buy a last-minute ticket. There were no tickets left at the box office — none at all. I asked three different staff members, but none of them could help. There was no way in without a valid ticket. I checked the official app, but there were no seats available in any section. I had not booked in advance because I thought there would be any number of tickets left on the night. I was wrong. There was no sign of anyone selling tickets outside, either, and none of the touts who are usually around were there. In the end, I went home with no concert, no ticket, and no good excuse for not planning ahead.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả một ngày mà bạn không có đủ thứ bạn cần — thời gian, tiền bạc, năng lượng — và cách bạn đối phó. Dùng no, none, any.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: No time, no money, none of the, not any, no idea, there was no
writing_prompts.example: There are days when nothing goes according to plan. Last Monday was one of them. I had no time for breakfast because the alarm did not go off. I had no money on my card because I had forgotten to transfer my salary. None of the buses came on time, and there was no one at the information desk at the station. By midday I had no energy left and no idea how I was going to get through the afternoon. There was no point feeling sorry for myself, though. I borrowed some money from a colleague, ate a sandwich at my desk, and worked steadily through my list. None of the tasks were finished perfectly, but none of them were left completely undone either. Some days, that is the best you can do.

intermediate.title: Another, other, others, the other, the others
intermediate.content:
📌 ANOTHER
• Dùng như determiner (trước noun) hoặc pronoun (không có noun)
• Luôn đi với danh từ đếm được số ít

I need another cup of coffee. (determiner)
I've already had one beer, but I'll have another. (pronoun)

✦ Nghĩa: một cái khác / thêm một cái
❌ another students → ✅ another student / other students
📌 OTHER
• Dùng như determiner trước danh từ số nhiều hoặc danh từ không đếm được
• KHÔNG dùng như pronoun (→ dùng others)

I want to talk to other doctors. (determiner + plural noun)
Do you have any other information? (determiner + uncountable noun)

❌ I want to talk to others doctors. → ✅ other doctors
📌 OTHERS
• Dùng như pronoun — KHÔNG theo sau bởi noun
• Nghĩa: những người/vật khác

I don't want these glasses. Aren't there any others?
Some students passed; others failed.
A lot of volunteers joined, but we need to find others.
📌 THE OTHER
• Dùng như determiner + singular noun HOẶC như pronoun
• Nghĩa: cái còn lại (trong 2) hoặc phần còn lại cụ thể

I have two cats. One is black; the other is white. (pronoun)
The other computer is about five years old. (determiner)
You can also say the other one.
📌 THE OTHERS
• Dùng như determiner + plural noun HOẶC như pronoun
• Nghĩa: những người/vật còn lại (trong một nhóm cụ thể)

Joel and Karen are here, but where are the other kids? (determiner)
Joel and Karen are here, but where are the others? (pronoun)
📐 Bảng tổng hợp
TừDeterminer?Pronoun?Số
another✓ (+ singular)✓Số ít
other✓ (+ plural/uncountable)✗Số nhiều / uncountable
others✗✓Số nhiều
the other✓ (+ singular)✓Số ít (còn lại trong 2)
the others✓ (+ plural)✓Số nhiều (còn lại trong nhóm)

exercises.q: This coffee is cold. Can I have ___ cup, please?
exercises.exp: another + singular noun = thêm một cái

exercises.q: Some people enjoyed the film, but ___ thought it was too long.
exercises.exp: others = pronoun (= other people), không theo sau noun

exercises.q: I have two pens. One is red; ___ is blue.
exercises.exp: the other = cái còn lại trong 2

exercises.q: There's no ___ way to do this. It's the only option.
exercises.exp: other + uncountable noun (way) = không có cách nào khác

exercises.q: Three students have arrived. Where are ___?
exercises.exp: the others = những người còn lại trong nhóm đã xác định

exercises.q: I don't like this design. Can you show me ___ one?
exercises.exp: another one = một cái khác (thêm)

exercises.q: Some books are available online; ___ you need to order.
exercises.exp: others = pronoun (= other books)

exercises.q: ___ students in the class found the test quite easy.
exercises.exp: Other + plural noun = students khác

exercises.q: One of the twins passed the exam; ___ failed.
exercises.exp: the other = cái còn lại trong 2 (twins)

exercises.q: I've finished my sandwich. Is there ___?
exercises.exp: another = pronoun (= another sandwich), standalone

exercises.q: Some of the volunteers left early; ___ stayed to help clean up.
exercises.exp: the others = những người còn lại trong nhóm đã xác định

exercises.q: She has two brothers. ___ is an architect and ___ is a nurse.
exercises.opts: One / the other,Another / the other,The other / one,Other / others
exercises.exp: One...the other = dùng cho đúng 2 người

exercises.q: Do you have ___ information about the programme?
exercises.exp: any other + uncountable noun = thêm thông tin nào không

exercises.q: Five students left the room. The teacher asked ___ to remain seated.
exercises.exp: the others = những người còn lại (cụ thể trong lớp)

exercises.q: Please give me ___ chance. I won't make the same mistake again.
exercises.exp: another chance = thêm một cơ hội (singular)

exercises.q: This shirt doesn't fit. Is there ___ in a larger size?
exercises.exp: another = pronoun (= another shirt)

exercises.q: Some problems are easy to solve; ___ require more thought.
exercises.exp: others = pronoun = other problems

exercises.q: She moved the books from one shelf to ___, leaving this one empty.
exercises.exp: the other = cái kệ còn lại (trong 2)

exercises.q: I need ___ two weeks to finish the project properly.
exercises.exp: another + time/number expression = thêm (another two weeks = two more weeks)

exercises.q: ___ day, I want to visit Japan and experience the culture.
exercises.exp: Another day = một ngày nào đó trong tương lai

exercises.q: This pen has run out. Can I borrow ___ (another/other) one?
exercises.exp: another + singular → một cái khác

exercises.q: Some guests had already eaten; ___ (others/other) were still waiting.
exercises.exp: others = pronoun, không theo sau noun

exercises.q: I have two jackets. One is formal; ___ (the other/another) is casual.
exercises.exp: the other = cái còn lại trong 2

exercises.q: There are ___ (other/another) restaurants nearby if you don't like this one.
exercises.exp: other + plural noun

exercises.q: Six students have handed in their work. Where are ___ (the others/others)?
exercises.exp: the others = những người còn lại (cụ thể trong lớp)

exercises.q: Could I have ___ (another/other) glass of water, please?
exercises.exp: another + singular → thêm một ly

exercises.q: Some people prefer working alone; ___ (others/other) prefer teamwork.
exercises.exp: others = pronoun

exercises.q: There is ___ (another/no other) way to solve this problem. We must accept it.
exercises.exp: no other way = không có cách nào khác (other + singular uncountable)

exercises.q: I need ___ (another/other) three days to complete the assignment.
exercises.exp: another + number + plural noun = thêm ... nữa

exercises.q: Two of the four students passed. ___ (The others/Others) need to retake the exam.
exercises.exp: the others = hai người còn lại (trong nhóm đã xác định)

exercises.q: We visited three museums. One was free; ___ (the others/others) charged entry.
exercises.exp: the others = những cái còn lại (trong 3 đã xác định)

exercises.q: She has two brothers. One lives in Paris; ___ (the other/another) lives in Rome.
exercises.exp: the other = người còn lại trong 2

exercises.q: I've tried several approaches. Maybe I should try a completely ___ (other/another) method.
exercises.exp: another method = phương pháp hoàn toàn khác

exercises.q: Some of the parcels arrived today; ___ (the others/others) should arrive tomorrow.
exercises.exp: the others = những gói còn lại (đã xác định)

exercises.q: This film was brilliant. I'd love to watch ___ (other/another) one by the same director.
exercises.exp: another + singular = thêm một bộ phim khác

exercises.q: He has read all the books on the shelf except ___ (the other/the others) two at the back.
exercises.exp: the other + number + noun = hai quyển còn lại

exercises.q: ___ (Other/Another) students in the class had already been told about the change.
exercises.exp: Other + plural noun (một số học sinh khác)

exercises.q: We waited for ___ (another/other) hour before the meeting finally started.
exercises.exp: another + singular noun = thêm một giờ nữa

exercises.q: Some countries have adopted the policy; ___ (others/the others) remain sceptical.
exercises.exp: others = pronoun, không xác định (một số nước khác chung chung)

exercises.q: They shook hands with each ___ (other/another) after the difficult negotiation.
exercises.exp: each other = reciprocal pronoun (cụm cố định)

Five students left and the teacher asked the others to stay behind

One twin loves sport while the other prefers reading and music

The supermarket is on the other side of the road not this one

passage.text: We visited three museums during our trip to the capital. The first was the National History Museum, which was fascinating. {{1}} was the Museum of Modern Art, which neither of us had visited before. {{2}} museum we went to was smaller but surprisingly interesting. We spent {{3}} two hours there than we had planned because there was so much to see. Some exhibits were educational; {{4}} were thought-provoking in {{5}} ways. I had studied art history at school, but this trip gave me a completely {{6}} perspective. My friend, who had {{7}} plans for the afternoon, suggested we visit {{8}} gallery in the same building. It was free, so we had nothing to lose. We found {{9}} visitors had already left, but {{10}} were still browsing the upper floor. In {{11}} room there was a sculpture that stopped us both in our tracks. We bought postcards at the shop — I chose one; she chose {{12}}.

Learning a second language takes time, dedication, and patience. Some people find it easy; {{1}} struggle enormously. I started learning Italian three years ago. At first, I tried {{2}} method — apps, textbooks, group classes — but nothing seemed to work. {{3}} people in my class made rapid progress; I was not one of them. I decided to try {{4}} approach: one-to-one lessons with a native speaker. This changed everything. My tutor showed me {{5}} techniques that no textbook had covered. She would correct one mistake and then move on to {{6}}. There were days when I made {{7}} kind of error repeatedly, but she was patient. After {{8}} year of lessons, I could hold a basic conversation. {{9}} students in my original class had given up by that point. I kept going. Now I am planning {{10}} trip to Italy and I can manage on my own.

There were twelve students in the seminar group. The tutor asked two of them to present first. {{1}} ten waited quietly. The first presentation was confident and well-structured. {{2}} one was shorter but more original. When both had finished, {{3}} students asked questions. Some were straightforward; {{4}} required careful thought. The tutor then split the group into pairs. One pair worked on a case study; {{5}} pairs worked on {{6}} tasks. In my pair, one of us led the discussion and {{7}} took notes. At the end, the tutor said: 'Some of your ideas were excellent. {{8}} need more development.' She encouraged us to explore {{9}} perspectives and not rely on {{10}} single approach. It was a long session, but we all felt we had learned something valuable.

I have been to two writing workshops this year. The first focused on fiction; {{1}} was about non-fiction and memoir. Both were useful, but in completely {{2}} ways. In the fiction workshop, some participants wrote short stories; {{3}} experimented with poetry. Our tutor introduced one technique each session and then moved on to {{4}}. I found some exercises straightforward; {{5}} challenged me more than I expected. In {{6}} workshop, the focus was on voice and structure. The tutor said that {{7}} writers rely too heavily on {{8}} people's stories and forget their own. She encouraged us to try {{9}} approach — writing from memory rather than imagination. By the end, I had produced two pieces I was genuinely proud of and had signed up for {{10}} course the following term.

Our neighbourhood has two community gardens. One is near the school; {{1}} is behind the library. Both are run by volunteers. Some volunteers come every weekend; {{2}} drop in only occasionally. There are always tasks to do — one group weeds and waters; {{3}} plant new seeds or maintain the paths. Last spring we organised {{4}} project: building raised beds for wheelchair users. Some materials were donated; we had to buy {{5}}. It took {{6}} three weekends to finish. {{7}} volunteers joined us halfway through, which was very welcome. When one bed was completed, we started on {{8}}. By the end, we had built four in total. {{9}} two are already in use. The {{10}} will be ready by summer. It was a wonderful example of what people can do when they work together.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về hai lựa chọn bạn đã cân nhắc trong cuộc sống — một cái bạn đã chọn và cái còn lại bạn đã bỏ qua
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Another, other, the other: one option… the other; no other; another way
writing_prompts.example: When I finished school, I had to choose between two very different paths. One was to study engineering at a top university; the other was to pursue art at a design college. I had no other options that genuinely excited me at that time. Many of my friends were going into engineering; others were choosing creative courses. I chose engineering because it offered more security, but I have always wondered about the other path. I have since found other ways to express my creativity — I paint in my spare time and have taken another course in digital design. One day, I hope to combine both interests. There is always another opportunity to explore a different direction.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một bữa tiệc hoặc sự kiện — ai đã đến, ai không đến, và những gì đã xảy ra với những người/vật còn lại
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: The others, others, the other, another in event/group contexts
writing_prompts.example: We organised a leaving party for a colleague who was moving abroad. About twenty people were invited, but only fourteen came. The others sent messages to say they were unable to make it. Some guests arrived on time; others came later in the evening. Two colleagues brought food — one made a Vietnamese dish; the other made a salad. Some people stayed for the whole evening; the others left around nine. We drank and talked for another two hours after the food was finished. Some of us took photos; others just enjoyed the moment. At the end of the night, we all realised there was no other group of people we would rather spend an evening with.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về kinh nghiệm học một kỹ năng mới — những phương pháp bạn đã thử, những khó khăn bạn gặp phải, và cách bạn tiến bộ
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Another method/approach/attempt; other ways/people; others struggled too; the other option
writing_prompts.example: Last year I decided to learn how to cook proper Thai food. At first I tried one method — copying recipes from the internet — but the results were disappointing. I looked for another approach and found a local cooking class. The teacher showed us other techniques that no recipe had explained. Some students in the class had cooked Thai food before; others, like me, were complete beginners. One week we learned to make curry; the other weeks focused on soups and noodles. There was always another ingredient to discover. Some dishes came out well; others needed more practice. I had no other option but to keep trying. After three months, I had developed other skills too — knife work, time management in the kitchen, and a better understanding of flavour balance.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả ngày làm việc hoặc học tập của bạn — các nhiệm vụ khác nhau, chuyển từ việc này sang việc khác
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Another task/meeting/class; other colleagues/students; the other part/half; others in the team
writing_prompts.example: My working day usually starts with emails and then moves on to other tasks. I try to complete one report before moving on to another. Some of my colleagues work independently; the others collaborate in pairs. I often spend the first half of the day in meetings and the other half at my desk. When one project is finished, there is always another waiting. Some tasks are routine; others require creative thinking. Yesterday I had no other option but to stay late to meet a deadline. The others in my team had already left, so I worked alone. I finished at eight o'clock and treated myself to a takeaway. Another long day, but a productive one.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về hai cuốn sách hoặc hai bộ phim bạn đã đọc/xem gần đây — so sánh chúng và giải thích bạn muốn đọc/xem thêm gì
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: The other (book/film), another (recommendation), others (in the series), other (characters/themes)
writing_prompts.example: I recently read two novels by the same author. The first was a thriller set in Tokyo; the other was a quieter, more reflective story about family. Both were excellent, but in completely different ways. The first had no other flaw than a slightly rushed ending. The other was slow to start but deeply rewarding. The other characters in the second book were more fully developed. After finishing both, I immediately looked for another book by the same writer. Others in my reading group had already read three of her novels; I had read only those two. Some members preferred the fast-paced ones; others liked the more literary works. I would recommend either to anyone looking for intelligent, well-crafted fiction.
